title: Smoky Roasted Irish Pak Choy Stir-fry
description: This spicy and crunchy weeknight dish features roasted pak choi with a smoky kick, perfect for those busy evenings when you need something quick yet flavorful.

ingredients.name: Honey or Molasses
ingredients.notes: To balance the smokiness and heat

instructions: Wash the Irish Pak Choy and trim off any thicker stems. Remove the outer leaves if desired.
Toss the chopped shallots, garlic, chili flakes, honey/molasses, smoked paprika powder, and sesame seeds in a small mixing bowl until evenly combined.
Place the chopped pak choi on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush lightly with olive oil.
Spread the mixed spice mixture evenly over the pak choy leaves.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly crispy on the outside.
While the pak choi is roasting, prepare a quick stir-fry sauce by heating another tablespoon of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add more garlic and shallots to sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant. Then add the roasted pak choy leaves, squeezing out any excess liquid if necessary.
Pour in the stir-fry sauce mixture and toss everything together well with more olive oil if needed for a glossy finish.
Serve hot as a side dish or on its own with steamed rice for a complete meal.
